task_updates.executor_notes: Validation completed in the required order with `PYTHONPATH=.`: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py` passed (`20 passed, 1 warning`), `pytest tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py` passed (`35 passed, 1 warning`), and `pytest tests/` passed end-to-end (`79 passed, 1 warning`). No unrelated pre-existing failures surfaced; the plan's `baseline_test_failures` field is `null`, and the full suite remained green. A throwaway reproducer script at `/tmp/harden_payment_cap_repro.py` exercised the adversarial capped `amount_token` path with `rolling_usd=1900`, `amount_token=1.0`, provider price `150.0`, and `daily_usd_cap=2000`; it reproduced the hardened behavior end-to-end by returning a `manual_hold` row with `last_error='rolling daily cap exceeded: $2050.00 > $2000.00'` and persisted `amount_usd` / `token_price_usd` in both the row and `request_payload`, then the script was deleted. Manual read-through audit confirmed the fail-closed invariants remain intact: `PaymentService.request_payment` performs collision detection before idempotent return, only returns active same-wallet prior rows idempotently, creates `manual_hold` on failed/cancelled slot reuse with a different wallet, blocks active different-wallet collisions by returning `None` after `_emit_cap_breach(prior_row)`, stamps derived USD only inside the capped non-test `amount_token` branch, fails closed to `manual_hold` when token pricing is unavailable, and `execute_payment` still uses the stored `recipient_wallet` string. Both wallet-update callers remain fail-closed: `admin_chat_cog._handle_wallet_received` catches `WalletUpdateBlockedError`, notifies admin review, and returns before advancing the intent; `grants_cog._start_payment_flow` catches the same exception, posts the thread warning, and returns before requesting payment. Follow-up `rg -n "amount_usd" src tests` found the new non-null `amount_usd` usage only in tests, the rolling-24h helper, admin payment serialization, grant USD-originated payment creation, and PaymentCog success-DM formatting/thresholding; no downstream consumer was found that explicitly depends on token-denominated payouts keeping `amount_usd is None`. Git-history audit findings from T3 remain unchanged: historical hits showed env-var lookups / `Keypair.from_bytes(key_bytes)` but no literal secrets, no `.env`/`secrets` history, no `sk-...` tokens, and `gitleaks` was unavailable. Log audit findings from T11 remain unchanged: secret/key-material greps only found env lookups, logger greps found no env/private-key/secret emission path, and no leak was detected.
